title: Phormium orchioides
taxonomy: Phormium orchioides is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae. It is commonly known as the 'orchid phormium' or 'New Zealand flax'. This plant is native to New Zealand and is a member of the genus Phormium. Phormium orchioides is closely related to Phormium cookianum and Phormium colensoi, and is often confused with them due to their similar appearances. The exact classification of Phormium orchioides is still being studied and confirmed by botanists.
uses: Phormium orchioides, also known as the "orchid flax," has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for various purposes, including wound healing and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and roots are said to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating infections and reducing inflammation. In traditional Maori medicine, Phormium orchioides was also used to treat digestive issues and as a diuretic.
